title: A unique and unforgettable landscape
text:
Visiting the Okavango Delta was unlike any other safari experience. The mix of water channels, islands, and open floodplains creates a constantly changing landscape.

Each outing felt different depending on the light and wildlife activity. The peaceful atmosphere, especially in the early mornings, made the experience feel immersive and unhurried.

It’s a remarkable destination for experiencing Botswana’s wilderness.

title: An unforgettable lifetime African Safari
text:
We are truly delighted to welcome you and to share our best experience with African Unique Wilderness Safaris on Botswana soil, the Okavango Delta.
Based in Maun, at the gateway to this remarkable wilderness, the team together with their director Joel (aka  Joel Of Africa) is committed to offering authentic, personalised safari experiences that connect guests with the beauty, wildlife, and culture of Botswana. From the moment you arrive, they ensure you feel safe, comfortable, and inspired by the unique landscapes and wildlife that make the Okavango Delta so special.
We truly recommend African Unique Wilderness for the unforgettable memories in the heart of Africa.
Warm regards,

title: What an experience!
text:
We did a 3 day glamping safari and it was totally amazing.
We saw a leopard with a fresh antelope kill, lions 10m away, experienced the hippo highway up close, elephants crossing the river in front of us, giraffes and more.

title: Booking Mokoro trip to Okavango on cheep.
text:
We bypassed travel agencies and booked mokoro 1 day trip directly with Okavango Kopano Mokoro Community Trust (OKMCT) office. This avoids travel agencies and their hefty fees. OKMCT office is on Sir Seretse Khama Rd about 5 km outside of Maun (google will help you to find your way there). We went there a day before our mokoro trip.

Fees per day are as follows:
P105 per vehicle for concession entrance fee.
P315 for hiring Head Poler (paid directly to the poler at landing)
P105 for renting mokoro (2 people fits in)
P105 camping fee (if you stay overnight)

If you have 4x4 vehicle then you pay above fees, collect voucher/ permit at OKMCT office and just drive to the landing to start your mokoro trips. It takes about 90 minutes drive to get from Maun to mokoro  landing. Most of the people start driving from Maun at 7:30AM and get on water shortly after 9AM.

We had to hire a driver with 4x4 vehicle for 1 day to drive us from our Maun hotel to mokoro landing. OKMCT person helped with this as a side private arrangement.  This cost us  additional BWP1,700. We also bought lunch for ourselves and poler. 

So, all together we spent BWP2,330 for 2 people 1 day mokoro trip. It would have been only BWP630 if we had our own 4x4 vehicle.

title: Botswana's Okavango Delta is brilliant in March
text: March seems a very good time to visit the Delta.  Very green.  Drying out from rains of January and February. Temperatures delightful day and night and perfect for a camping safari, with clear skies brimming with stars by night, trees with flocks of all sorts of gorgeously feather birds, wildflowers, and of course, hippos, elephants, crocs, leopards, lions, zebras, impalas, meerkats, hyena, ostrichs, monkeys, wild dogs. We hadn't realised the dugouts had been superseded with fibreglass kayaks a decade ago, but from a conservation point of view this makes sense.

title: MOBILE CAMPING IN THE OKAVANGO DELTA AND CHOBE NATIONAL PARK ARE THE BEST IN BOTSWANA.
text: AMAZING, PLACE YOU DON'T WANNA MISS WHEN YOU ARE A TRUE NATURE LOVER, I STRONGLY RECCOMEND TRAVELERS TO DO CAMPING IN THE OKAVANGO DELTA, BECAUSE IT IS QUITE AFFODABLE FOR A LIFETIME.

title: Worth seeing and impressive nature paradise
text: During our tour of Namibia and Botswana we spent a total of 5 days in two different accommodations in the Okavango Delta and were able to enjoy this wonderful region to the fullest during this time. The most impressive were the impressions you could enjoy from the water, as there were so many different bird species. We took part in several boat tours where the guides showed us the most diverse animal species.

title: Wonderful place to visit
text: Go in the wet seasons when you can go in a mokoro canoe down the canals the elephants create by walking through the tall grasses. Amazing place to be.

title: Amazing
text: This is the most visited place in botswana.we stayed at kwara camp.very friendly staffs.stayed here for three nights.whats an amazing experience.

title: A great place to camp and experience some African outdoors
text: Camped here for a couple nights on a group tour. Activities included poler transport to and from the campsite, a couple walks around the delta, a poler cruise along the waterways at dusk, a swim in the waterways, and songs around the camp fire at night. A great experience, even including the time we nearly got charged by an elephant that was startled by our approach (but our poler handled it fine). One of the highlights of our Africa tour.

title: The real Africa
text:
The Okavango originates in Angola, for a short stretch it marks the borders between Angola itself and Namibia, then flows into Botswana, within the Moremi.

It has a unique characteristic: it does not flow into any sea and does not flow into any river: it simply disperses in a large swamp called a delta.

We stayed inside the Moremi nature reserve for eight days, in two different tented camps, from which our safaris in search of animals started daily.

We were particularly lucky, having found lions, a large quantity of elephants, hippos, zebras and gazelles, the very rare leopards; many different varieties of birds.

The tented camps, rather than being tiring, allowed us to live in complete contact with nature, in an "out of this world" situation: electricity only that strictly necessary to light dinner, limited water for washing (no problem for that to be drinking), Spartan tents, no telephone contact or Internet (finally!!): an experience to be repeated and recommended.

Nature, during the period of our stay, was still asleep, waiting for the heavy rains that arrive between the end of November and the beginning of December.

In many trips we have taken, none have given us the emotions of the Okavango: spectacular!!!

title: Beautiful Okavango
text: Okavango delta is a very beautiful place I visited the area on a mokoro safari which was a great way to explore the area although it was a very hot day when I explored

title: Overflight with small plane was the sensation
text: We took off from Maun in a small plane for a sightseeing flight over the delta. We finally saw green Africa despite the rainy season and large herds of all kinds of animals. Fantastic.

title: The outstanding Okavango Delta
text: Our first ever Safari Trip. Stayed 3 nights at Kwara Camp. Excellent facilities and welcoming staff. The game drives were superb and we saw incredible wildlife in the most beautiful and unspoilt landscape. Highly recommended

title: SIMPLE SPLENDOUR
text: The 1000th World Heritage Site, the area covers more than two million hectares of wetlands and seasonally flooded grasslands. The Moremi Game Reserve is part of the delta. It is an excellent place to take a mokoro ride, the edges of the boats but inches above the waterline, skillfully guided by boatmen. The game viewing is very good.

title: Marvellous camping Safari in Botswana.
text: The 9-day 8-night Okavango Expeditions camping Safari, Kasane to Maun, with 2 nights in 4 different  campsites, starting in Chobe, was a marvellous experience.  Tour guides/drivers Paul & Ossi had superb fauna/flora, knowledge, binocular-grade eyesight &, seemingly a 6th sense on where to find elusive animals.  The food was amazing, especially considering the prep. conditions and the team setting up and taking down the en-suite (bush style!)  tents, with beds, were streamlined and speedy - an all-round excellent experience.  Sitting under the star-studded sky, with lions grunting not far off, plus other animal communications during the night, just added to the experience.

title: incredible
text: Wenn Afrika-Safari, dann nur Botswana. Ist zwar schweinemäßig teuer, aber das absolut Beste von allem. Da kommt Kenia nicht mit, auch nicht Südafrika und auch nicht Krüger Park. Hier sind die Tiere und hier ist das ursprüngliche Afrika

title: Prachtig
text: Dit vergeet je nooit meer! Wonderschone ervaring van de natuur met veel wildlife, wandelingen, zonsopgang en -ondergang,..

title: Seeing African wildlife in style!
text: We followed a pack of wild dogs on a kill and saw many other animals. We stayed at Duke's Camp which was amazing!

title: Qualcosa di unico....
text:
Il Delta è un ecosistema unico situato al centro di una delle maggiori distese di sabbia del mondo, il Bacino del Kalahari, proprio come un’oasi in un territorio inospitale. La vegetazione è lussureggiante e l’avifauna è tra le più ricche dell’Africa. Il fiume Okavango, non raggiunge mai il mare, ma le sue possenti acque si riversano sulle sabbie del Kalahari. Qui la grande sete del deserto viene localmente placata in una selva blu-verde di acqua dolce, con canneti color smeraldo e alberi imponenti.
Il fiume Okavango ha la propria sorgente nell'Altopiano del Benguela in Angola e prima di penetrare in Botswana attraversa circa 1.300 km, poi si allarga e si disperde formando tre corsi principali ed un infinito numero di ramificazioni: canali, pozze e lagune di acqua incredibilmente limpida e pura che coprono una superficie di circa 16.000 kmq. I paesaggi dell'Okavango, le oltre 600 specie di animali e uccelli che vi abitano, la vita acquatica, tutto rende questa parte del continente africano, totalmente sconosciuta ai più, una delle mete più straordinarie. In questo connubio di terra e acqua la natura trionfa: pellicani, egrette, cicogne, aquile pescatrici, pesci tigre, coccodrilli, ippopotami, elefanti e anche, con un po’ di fortuna, predatori.

title: What Botswana is known for and it doesn't disappoint!
text: We explored the Okavango delta with Sanctuary Stanley's camp during the dry season (late May) and I have to say that the Okavango delta is one of the most beautiful areas I've ever seen in Africa.  The amount of animals and the variety of animals that we saw...well, we've never seen that many before.  And when the rains come and the entire area floods, the same animals are still there, but their habits/behaviors change accordingly.  So we're longing to go back during the rainy season to see the entire area again...but completely new to us again.  Definitely go here!!

title: Three nights at Kwara Camp
text: Three superb days and nights at Kwara Camp in the Okavango Delta. Excellent amenities, wonderful staff, astonishing wildlife. Highly recommended. One of the best places in Africa.

title: Wow
text:
Stayed here for 2 nights as part of an Intrepid trip. Have to say this surpassed my expectations. We were taken to the campsite in Mokoros by local villagers. Once there the campsite was set up for us.
We done bush walking where we were taught about the local wildlife. Right outside the camp site we had elephants and hippos. On the first night I was awoken by elephants passing through the camp!
We bathed in a natural pool right by our camp.
This was definitely the highlight of my 2-week trip.

title: We explored Okavango Delta with Sekanka Safari
text: We spend a good week on a safari starting from Maun, ending in Kasane / Victoria Falls and loved the Xakanax area in Okavango Delta most. We opted for a tailor-made tented safari through a company called "Sekanka Safari", which we can recommend to anybody looking for a small safari company to take their family on the safari of a life time. When looking at safaris we wanted to do it all: Okavango Delta, Chobe, Moreni, Savuti, Victoria Falls, under the assumption that the areas would be very different from each other. In reality they are not that different allthough we found the Xakanaxa area to be the most stunning of all. We were very lucky with the game sights we had there and perhaps that helps with our preference for that area. After those, the game ride in Chobe felt very average. I know that sounds very snobbish but after such an amazing start of our safari tour I guess we were spoiled ;-).

title: Spectacular
text:
Amazing experience to spend 2 nights in the delta. No facilities in this untouched area.
The campsite was enough for a group, able to swim in the river (with no chemical used), had bush walks, studied tracks etc.

title: Amazing wildlife
text:
The Delta has been on my wife’s bucket list for many years, so it was great that we finally managed to visit and, due to covid, with relatively few tourists. Apart from our game drives, we decided to take a scenic flight (with Major Blue Air out of Maun) which is great if you don’t mind a small plane and a few bumps here and there. 

We saw all of wildlife we’d hoped for as well as getting a much better appreciation of the delta.

title: Imperdible
text: Experiencia para al menos una vez en la vida, aunque no hay mucha agua. Puesta de sol, preciosa. Muchos insectos.

title: Captivating
text: What a treasure the Okavango is! Serenely beautiful and peaceful,  and truly something to protect and enjoy.  Lots of animals and birds to see, gorgeous landscapes, and unforgettable experiences. Protect it!

title: Assolutamente da fare
text:
Bellissima esperienza, abbiamo sorvolato il Delta Okavango, tanti branchi di animali, scenario molto bello , ogni angolo é un paradiso, una distesa a perdita d occhio, consiglio, dopo questo abbiamo navigato con la lancia Mokoro sul Okavango, abbiamo fatto un trekking di 10 km all interno , tanti ippopotami, zebre, impala e ci siamo fermati a dormire nelle tende, un esperienza da provare , solo
grilli e il suono degli ippopotami.

title: Awesome safari
text: Game drives and boat rides with experienced guides made the entire experience an unforgettable trip.

title: WATERY WORLD WITH ANIMALS
text: An amazing place. Lots of animals to see. There were several people in our ad hoc group. We had a tour guide with us who was knowledgeable and spoke three languages,  including English.

title: Hope to come again…
text:
So diverse… from speed boat to mokoro to game drive to horse riding. And a few more….
And it changes, too. So different in Summer or May-July…

title: Nowhere better to go on Safari than Botswana's Okavango
text: The best safari experiences in Africa can be had in the Okavango Delta. Doesn't matter if you are at a lodge, mobile luxury camping or basic camping, canoeing or horseriding, you'll have the best time of  your life.

title: Anyone seen an elephant?  Here they are!
text: More animals in one day than you can imagine!  Botswana is the best!  We were with Arifcan Bush Camps.  Superb!

title: Serene, enchanting place.
text: It was amazing to be in such a special place that changes so much based on the annual rainfall and water seasons. Quiet, peaceful, solitary. The stars were amazing!!! It was so fun to camp out in the bush, listening to our neighboring hippos! The makoro rides are fun and the walking safaris were humbling and insightful, though the animals kept their distance. We barely entered the edge of the Delta and would love to go deeper into the Delta next time. Our experience was heightened by the possibility that the Okavango might not get water forever because of increased water consumption in Angola. This amazing place will be greatly changed, so go there now before its gone. Botswana is an amazing country with friendly and welcoming people!

title: TOP
text:
Uno de los lugares mas especiales donde he estado. Cuando Estuve yo, no estava Nada masificado. 
Vimos leopardos, guepardos, elefantes, 18 leones juntos, hipopotamos, cocodrilos, ….todo lo que nos habiamos programado. 
Me guataría volver.

title: One of the natural wonders.
text: This natural wonderland must be on every nature and wildlife travellers bucket list. The rainwaters flow from Angola through Namibia into this pan shaped delta into a myriad of lakes and bull-rush waterways.

title: Letaka Safaris - mobile tented safaris
text:
We had an absolutely amazing six-night journey from beginning to end. Mmegi met us at the airport and took us to our pre-Safari destination, then collected us again and made sure we were airport ready… we arrived in Moremi for a 3-night stay, and was met by our guide Lucas, it was nonstop excitement from the word go, we saw a small pride of male lions before we even arrived at camp. and then drove to Khwai for 3 nights. Each location had a different feel and experience. Our Guide, Lucas was extremely knowledgeable and did not pass an opportunity to turn every occasion into an adventure, from spotting and chasing a leopard, not just once but twice! To leading us to a herd of 500+ strong buffalo, not once but twice, different locations. We saw elephants, lions, loads of hippos, giraffes, zebra, warthogs, wildebeest, African wild cat, wild dogs, Lechwe and a rare buck called the Roan antelope. Not to mention all the birds, flora and crocodiles. The best part was 2 mating African rock pythons. The river Mokoro excursion in Khwai was just awesome. I also enjoyed the walking safari.

The camping facilities are fantastic, way more than I expected. Elliot delivered a warm shower to our en suite every afternoon and warm water to wash with every morning. Elliot and KT had our tents and camp cleaned and we had a surprise towel origami on our beds every day. This all in the African mobile bush safari!

Now the best part !!!! the food - was astounding what they created on a camp fire. KK cooked up three course dinners every night plus lunches and warm breakfasts. A freshly baked cake every day, felt like I was in a 5-star hotel! Lucas had wonderful stories to share at dinner time, and around the campfire.

Coming back from a drive, we would be welcomed with a welcome song, smiles, a cold drink and warm washcloths for our faces and hands. And a blazing campfire.

Mmegi made sure we were covid tested before we said our goodbyes…

Thank you Letaka Safaris and team for sharing your playground with us for a short while…

title: A massive water expanse - a must to see
text: We spent 3 nights on the delta camping and doing a mokoro ride. This is a must experience.  The size of the delta is not easy to comprehend. It is worth doing a helicopter flight over the delta as well as boating.

title: Great sightings of lions, leopards, wild dogs, elephants
text: Our guide Monti from Xakanaxa Camp was able to find 4 of the big 5 on our game drives in the Moremi Reserve,  Camp is very comfortable and the food excellent.

title: Beautiful area - so many animals
text: I loved my visit to the Delta.  I got to see so many different types of animals.  I have never seen such a variey of wildlife and the setting along the river was truly beautiful.  I loved the opportunity to spend time on water and land and would recommend it on any travel to Botswana.
